book_claims.claim: High oxalate content hinders calcium absorption
book_claims.mechanism: Oxalates chelate calcium forming insoluble calcium oxalate that cannot be absorbed in the intestine

supplementary_research.finding: Oxalate content of spinach (750-800 mg/100g) reduces calcium bioavailability to only 5%, compared to 27% from milk and 49-61% from kale/broccoli. Boiling reduces oxalate by 30-87% depending on method.
Dietary nitrate from beetroot juice and spinach significantly lowers blood pressure (4-5 mmHg systolic) via nitric oxide pathway. These vegetables are among the richest dietary nitrate sources.
Beetroot supplementation improves exercise performance by 1-3% and reduces oxygen cost of exercise by 5%. Mechanism is nitric oxide-mediated improvement in mitochondrial efficiency.

Dietary nitrate from beets and leafy greens reliably improves blood flow, lowers blood pressure, and enhances exercise efficiency. The oxalate concern is real for calcium but does not negate other nutritional benefits.

culinary_pairings.source: Fat enhances absorption of carotenoids and vitamin K from greens
Sauteed spinach or chard with garlic is universal; complementary anti-inflammatory compounds
Acid brightens earthy flavors; vitamin C enhances iron absorption from these iron-rich greens
Classic beet-goat cheese salad; tangy cheese balances beet sweetness
Omega-3 fats pair with nitrate-rich greens; textural contrast in salads
Classic seasoning for creamed spinach and chard gratins; masks bitterness
Sesame calcium complements these vegetables for calcium-conscious eaters; tahini beet dips

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Converted to nitric oxide via oral bacteria. Antiseptic mouthwash blocks this conversion. Peak blood levels 2-3 hours post-ingestion.
Bind calcium and may contribute to kidney stones in susceptible individuals. Boiling and discarding water reduces oxalates by 30-87%.
Unique pigment antioxidants not found in other vegetables. Give beets their red color. Anti-inflammatory and may protect LDL from oxidation.
Non-heme iron has 5-12% absorption. Vitamin C (from lemon juice) increases absorption 3-6x. Oxalates partially inhibit iron absorption too.
Spinach is one of the richest food sources. ~50% bioavailability vs synthetic folic acid.

synergies.description: Complement with low-oxalate vegetables for calcium needs
